todaysEvents.summary: US-Iran MOU formally signed at Burgenstock, Switzerland — formal ceremony Jun 19; Phase 2 nuclear talks begin immediately post-signing (Haaretz/SWI Jun 18-19)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: MOU digitally pre-signed Jun 18 at G7 Versailles; formal ceremony completed today Jun 19 at Burgenstock, Nidwalden. Pakistan PM Sharif and Qatar FM present as mediators. Deal terms: 60-day ceasefire + Iran dilutes enriched uranium under IAEA + US lifts sanctions + Iran oil sales resume + $300B reconstruction fund + Hormuz opens permanently toll-free. Phase 2 nuclear talks begin immediately after ceremony. Escalation level lowered to 2 (Elevated) — deal signed, ceasefire extended, active strikes halted.

todaysEvents.summary: Brent $79.34 (Jun 18 TradingEconomics, -0.3%); US markets closed Juneteenth; WTI $76.43; TTF €41.33 (-1.4%); iTraxx Asia IG ~139bp est. (Jun 19)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: Brent $79.34 (TradingEconomics Jun 18 confirmed, -0.27%; carried Jun 19 — US markets closed Juneteenth). WTI $76.43 (TradingEconomics Jun 18, -0.47%). TTF €41.33 (TradingEconomics Jun 18, -1.39%). iTraxx Asia IG ~139bp est. (-3bp on signing); ASEAN HY ~488bp est. (-7bp). EM FX stronger: USD/PHP ~55.75, USD/IDR ~15,900. ASEAN Banks outperforming on spread compression. Shipping equities rallying +2% on Hormuz reopening signal.

todaysEvents.summary: Deal signed — residual risk: nuclear sequencing, mine-clearing ~6 months from today, Ras Laffan restart late Aug 2026; Iran text disputes remain (Jun 19)
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Deal signed but implementation risks persist. Nuclear sequencing: Iran deputy FM says 60-day nuclear talks begin only after US releases frozen funds — creating sequencing tension. Mine-clearing ~6 months from today (Jun 19 signing); physical Hormuz traffic normalisation is a late-2026 event. Ras Laffan LNG restart not before late Aug 2026 regardless of deal timing. P&I clubs re-enter progressively post-mine clearance. Tail 10% — deal collapse risk lower now that formal signing complete.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: MOU formally signed Jun 19 Burgenstock. Ceasefire holds; Iran dilutes uranium under IAEA; US sanctions lifted; Iran oil sales resume. Hormuz mine-clearing begins (~6 months from Jun 19); P&I clubs re-enter progressively; Ras Laffan restart late Aug 2026. Brent normalises $70-80/bbl on supply resumption. Phase 2 nuclear talks in 60-day window proceed. Implementation friction from frozen-assets sequencing could delay but not collapse MOU. Probability raised to 62% on formal signing completion (Jun 19).

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Deal signed but implementation delays. Nuclear sequencing dispute (Iran withholds cooperation until frozen funds released) stalls mine-clearing; Hormuz physically inaccessible 60-90 days past signing. Ras Laffan slips to Q4 2026. Brent stays $80-95/bbl as market discounts partial supply resumption. iTraxx Asia IG settles 130-150bp; JKM holds $15-18/MMBtu. IRGC hardliner resistance to terms or US congressional opposition to sanctions relief are the main stress vectors.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Deal collapse post-signing. Iran hardliners or IRGC reject MOU implementation; nuclear sequencing terms break down; US Congress blocks sanctions relief; Israel unilateral strikes restart hostilities. Hormuz re-closed; Brent spikes $110-130/bbl; mine-clearing timeline extends 12+ months. Probability lowered to 10% from 15% (D107) on formal signing completion Jun 19. Key triggers: frozen-assets impasse causes Iran to withdraw; IDF unilateral action; US congressional veto.
